results.possibleIdentity: John E. Oppenheim was an engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, primarily associated with the Kliklok Corporation in Redwood City, California. At Kliklok, which was located at 2661 Spring Street, he specialized in electrical and mechanical design for automated packaging equipment. During this period, he was also a licensed amateur radio operator with the call sign K6CTZ (and formerly WN6CTY), residing in South San Francisco and Mountain View, areas adjacent to Redwood City. He is likely the same John E. Oppenheim who later earned a Ph.D. from the University of Michigan in 1983 with a dissertation on the federal response to natural disasters. This individual went on to a long career as an Assistant Director and methodology expert at the U.S. General Accounting Office (GAO), where he contributed to numerous reports on defense acquisitions, systems engineering, and public health policy through the late 1990s and 2000s.
